questions.text:
Based on the Red-Black tree depicted below, which case will be invoked when we insert node 65 into the tree?
Note 1: a node in bold is black and an underlined node is red.
Note 2: Don't forget nill nodes

Tree Structure:
- **40** (Black, Root)
- **20** (Black, Left Child of 40)
- _60_ (Red, Right Child of 40)
- **50** (Black, Left Child of 60)
- **80** (Black, Right Child of 60)
- _70_ (Red, Left Child of 80)
questions.explanation: When node 65 is inserted, it becomes the left child of node 70. Node 70 is red, creating a violation. The grandparent is 80, and the uncle is the right child of 80, which is a black NIL node. Since the uncle is black and the sequence 80 \(\rightarrow\) 70 \(\rightarrow\) 65 forms a 'line' (Left-Left relative to the grandparent's right child), this invokes **Case 3** of the insertion algorithm.

questions.text:
Based on the Red-Black tree depicted below, which case will be invoked when we insert node 10 into the tree?
Note 1: a node in bold is black and an underlined node is red.
Note 2: Don't forget nill nodes

Tree Structure:
- **40** (Black, Root)
- **20** (Black, Left Child of 40)
- _60_ (Red, Right Child of 40)
- **50** (Black, Left Child of 60)
- **80** (Black, Right Child of 60)
- _70_ (Red, Left Child of 80)
questions.explanation: Node 10 is inserted as the left child of node 20. Since node 20 is black (bold), inserting a red node as its child does not violate any Red-Black properties. Therefore, no fixing cases are invoked.

questions.text:
Based on the Red-Black tree depicted below, what is the height of the tree?
Note 1: a node in bold is black and an underlined node is red.
Note 2: Don't forget nill nodes

Tree Structure:
- **40** (Black, Root)
- **20** (Black, Left Child of 40)
- _60_ (Red, Right Child of 40)
- **50** (Black, Left Child of 60)
- **80** (Black, Right Child of 60)
- _70_ (Red, Left Child of 80)
questions.explanation: The height is the number of edges on the longest path from the root to a NIL node. The longest path is 40 \(\rightarrow\) 60 \(\rightarrow\) 80 \(\rightarrow\) 70 \(\rightarrow\) NIL. This path has 4 edges.

questions.text: The array {30, 25, 20, 22, 23, 18, 15, 10, 28, 5} is not a max-heap, but the max-heap properties are violated only once. Which two numbers (parent and child) in the array violate the heap properties?
questions.explanation: Checking indices (1-based): Index 4 (value 22) has children at index 8 (10) and index 9 (28). Since 22 < 28, the max-heap property is violated between parent 22 and child 28.

questions.text:
Based on the Red-Black tree depicted below, list the keys of the nodes using the pre-order traversal.
Note 1: a node in bold is black and an underlined node is red.
Note 2: Don't forget nill nodes

Tree Structure:
- **40** (Black, Root)
- **20** (Black, Left Child of 40)
- _60_ (Red, Right Child of 40)
- **50** (Black, Left Child of 60)
- **80** (Black, Right Child of 60)
- _70_ (Red, Left Child of 80)
questions.explanation:
Pre-order traversal follows: Root, Left, Right. 
1. Root: 40
2. Left: 20
3. Right: 60, its left child 50, its right child 80, and 80's left child 70.
Sequence: 40, 20, 60, 50, 80, 70.
Both functions are constants. Asymptotically, all constant functions belong to the same complexity class \( \Theta(1) \). Therefore, f is both an upper, lower, and tight bound for g.

questions.text: Given the array A <12, 11, 2, 7, 5, 14, 10, 8> what is the state of the array after applying the partition procedure in the Quick sort algorithm (Lomuto scheme) one time using the last element as pivot?
Given an array A with nodes: 10, 25, 35, 45, 55, 65, 75, 85, 5, 15, 20. After applying max-heapify at node 2 (value 25) the result will be:
questions.explanation: Node 2 (25) has children 4 (45) and 5 (55). Largest is 55. Swap 25 and 55. New position of 25 is index 5. Children of index 5 are 10 (15) and 11 (20). Since 25 > 15 and 25 > 20, heapify stops. Result: 10, 55, 35, 45, 25, 65, 75, 85, 5, 15, 20.

questions.text: The height \( h(x) \) of node x compared to its black-height \( bh(x) \) in a red black tree is:
questions.explanation: A fundamental property of Red-Black trees is that the height of any node is at most twice its black-height.

questions.text: Let \( T(n) \) be a function obeying the recurrence \( T(n) = 8T(n / 8) + \log n \), then \( T(n)= \)
questions.explanation: Master Theorem: \( a=8, b=8, f(n)=\log n \). \( n^{\log_8 8} = n \). Since \( \log n = O(n^{1-\epsilon}) \), this is Case 1. \( T(n) = \Theta(n) \).

questions.text:
Based on the Red-Black tree depicted below, what is the successor of node 60?
Note 1: a node in bold is black and an underlined node is red.
Note 2: Don't forget nill nodes

Tree Structure:
- **40** (Black, Root)
- **20** (Black, Left Child of 40)
- _60_ (Red, Right Child of 40)
- **50** (Black, Left Child of 60)
- **80** (Black, Right Child of 60)
- _70_ (Red, Left Child of 80)
questions.explanation: Successor is the minimum of the right subtree. Node 60's right subtree contains 80 and 70. The minimum value is 70.

questions.text: Given a heap that contains 512 nodes, the height of the heap rooted at level 6 is:
questions.explanation: A heap with 512 nodes has height 9 (log2 of 512). The height at level 6 is \( 9 - 6 = 3 \).
The outer loop runs n times. The function YYY(i) runs i/2 times for i \(\ge\) 10. The sum of i from 1 to n is \( \Theta(n^2) \).

questions.text: Which traversal method outputs the data in sorted order in a binary search tree:
questions.explanation: Inorder traversal (Left, Root, Right) always visits BST nodes in non-decreasing order.

questions.text: Given the recurrence relation \( T(n) = n + 3 \cdot T(n / 4) \), and using the recursion tree method, what is the number of nodes in the tree at level \( i = 3 \)?
questions.explanation: The number of nodes at level i is \( a^i \). Here \( a=3 \). For level 3, it is \( 3^3 = 27 \).

questions.text: A complete binary search tree has a worst case height of:
questions.explanation: A complete binary tree is balanced, so its height is always \( \Theta(\log n) \).

questions.text:
Based on the Red-Black tree depicted below, what is the black height (bh) of node 60?
Note 1: a node in bold is black and an underlined node is red.
Note 2: Don't forget nill nodes

Tree Structure:
- **40** (Black, Root)
- **20** (Black, Left Child of 40)
- _60_ (Red, Right Child of 40)
- **50** (Black, Left Child of 60)
- **80** (Black, Right Child of 60)
- _70_ (Red, Left Child of 80)
questions.explanation: Black-height is the number of black nodes on path to leaf (excluding the node itself). Path: 60 \(\rightarrow\) 50 (Black) \(\rightarrow\) NIL (Black). bh = 2.

questions.text: Insert the following elements into an initially empty binary min-heap: \( \langle 12, 14, 3, 16, 8, 7, 10 \rangle \). What data element is sitting in the right child of the left child of the root?
questions.explanation: Final min-heap array: [3, 8, 7, 16, 14, 12, 10]. Root is 3 (idx 1). Left child is 8 (idx 2). Right child of left child is idx 5, which is 14? Let's re-heap: 12 -> [12, 14] -> [3, 14, 12] -> [3, 14, 12, 16] -> [3, 8, 12, 16, 14] -> [3, 8, 7, 16, 14, 12] -> [3, 8, 7, 16, 14, 12, 10]. Idx 5 is 14. Actually, the question asks for right child of left child of root (idx 5). Let's check: 3(1), 8(2), 7(3), 16(4), 14(5). It is 14.

questions.text: Given the array \( A = \{18, 14, 6, 22, 4, 32, 12, 8\} \), after running the loop in the insertion sort algorithm five times, the result will be:

Based on the Red-Black tree depicted below, what is the depth of node 40?
Tree Structure: **40** (Root), **20** (Left), _60_ (Right).
questions.explanation: Depth is the distance from the root. Node 40 is the root, so depth is 0.

questions.text: In a Red-Black Tree, if node X is red and its uncle is also red, which case is triggered during insertion?
questions.explanation: Case 1 is triggered when the parent and uncle of the newly inserted red node are both red.

questions.text: In a Red-Black Tree insertion, if the uncle is black and the new node, parent, and grandparent form a 'triangle' (e.g., parent is left child, new node is right child), which case is triggered first?
questions.explanation: Case 2 involves a 'triangle' relationship with a black uncle, requiring a rotation to transform it into a Case 3 'line'.

questions.text: Given an array A: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30. After applying max-heapify at node 1 (value 5), what is the final array?
Solve the recurrence \( T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n \) using the Master Theorem:
questions.explanation: \( a=2, b=2, f(n)=n \). \( n^{\log_2 2} = n \). Since \( f(n) = \Theta(n) \), this is Case 2. \( T(n) = \Theta(n \log n) \).

questions.text: Given array <5, 3, 8, 4, 2, 7, 1, 6>, what is the state after one Lomuto partition using 6 as pivot?
questions.explanation: Pivot 6. Elements \(\le\) 6: 5, 3, 4, 2, 1. They move to the left. 8 and 7 move to the right. [5, 3, 4, 2, 1, 6, 8, 7].

questions.text: In Insertion Sort, after the first 3 elements of {10, 5, 2, 8} are processed, the array is:
For constants c and k, \( f(n) = c \) is \( \Theta(k) \) is:
questions.explanation: All non-zero constants belong to the same asymptotic complexity class \( \Theta(1) \).

questions.text: Which element is at the root of a min-heap after inserting 10, 20, 5, 15, 30?
questions.explanation: In a min-heap, the minimum element (5) always stays at the root.

questions.text: In a recursion tree for \( T(n) = 2T(n/2) + c \), how many nodes are at level 4?
questions.explanation: Number of nodes at level i is \( a^i = 2^4 = 16 \).

questions.text: What is the worst-case time complexity of searching in a balanced binary search tree?
questions.explanation: Balanced trees maintain a height of \( O(\log n) \), ensuring search time is logarithmic.
